questionHtml: Formulate two equations to show the amphiprotic nature of H2PO4−.
markschemeHtml:
H2PO4− (aq) + H+ (aq) → H3PO4 (aq) ✔

H2PO4− (aq) + OH− (aq) → HPO42− (aq) + H2O (l) ✔


Accept reactions of H2PO4− with any acidic, basic or amphiprotic species, such as H3O+, NH3 or H2O.

Accept H2PO4− (aq) → HPO42− (aq) + H+ (aq) for M2.

meta.title: Formulate two equations to show the amphiprotic nature of H2PO4−.
